title: Mondain the Wizard
summary: Evil wizard, Gem of Immortality, defeated 1355
content: Mondain was the son of King Wolfgang and sought power above all else. He murdered his father to obtain the Sun Ruby, which he transformed into the Gem of Immortality, making him invincible. Mondain enslaved all of Sosaria with dark magic. The Stranger was summoned to defeat him and used a time machine to travel back before the Gem's completion, shattering it and destroying Mondain. The shattering of the Gem caused a catastrophic sundering of reality, splitting Sosaria's four continents apart.

title: Minax the Enchantress
summary: Mondain's apprentice, revenge quest, time magic
content: Minax was Mondain's apprentice and lover who sought revenge against the Stranger for her master's death. She was a powerful enchantress who mastered time magic and created time doors for temporal warfare. Minax waged war across multiple timelines attempting to undo Mondain's defeat. She was eventually defeated by the Stranger in Ultima II.

title: Exodus
summary: Mondain+Minax creation, first AI, half-daemon half-machine
content: Exodus was the unholy creation of Mondain and Minax - the first artificial intelligence, half-daemon and half-machine. This mechanical being combined dark magic with ancient technology. Exodus waged war on Britannia until heroes destroyed it by removing mystical cards from its core. Exodus later returned in Ilshenar, corrupting constructs and golems.

title: Lord British
summary: First King of Britannia, created Eight Virtues
content: Lord British (Cantabrigian British) was the first King of Britannia and founder of the Eight Virtues system. He united the eight city-states under one banner and established the Great Council. Lord British was known for his wisdom, compassion, and near-immortality. He mysteriously disappeared from the realm, leaving the Council to rule in his absence. His friend Lord Blackthorn later became king.

title: Lord Blackthorn
summary: Philosopher of Chaos, friend/rival of British, current King
content: Lord Blackthorn was a friend and philosophical rival of Lord British. While British created the Eight Virtues, Blackthorn founded the philosophy of Chaos as a counterbalance. Though their followers fought in the streets through the Chaos and Order factions, British and Blackthorn remained friends. When British disappeared, Blackthorn grew bitter about not being named successor. He was corrupted (later revealed to be a facsimile) and waged war on Britannia, attacking Yew before being slain by Queen Dawn. The true Blackthorn was later resurrected and crowned as King of Britannia.

title: The Eight Virtues
summary: Honesty, Compassion, Valor, Justice, Sacrifice, Honor, Spirituality, Humility
content: The Eight Virtues form the moral foundation of Britannian society, established by Lord British. They are derived from three Principles: Truth (Lycaeum), Love (Empath Abbey), and Courage (Serpent's Hold). The virtues are: Honesty (Truth), Compassion (Love), Valor (Courage), Justice (Truth+Love), Sacrifice (Love+Courage), Honor (Truth+Courage), Spirituality (all three), and Humility (the foundation of all). Each virtue has an associated city and an opposing anti-virtue dungeon.

title: Britain - Capital of Britannia
summary: Capital city, Compassion, Castle Britannia
content: Britain is the capital and largest city of Britannia, representing the virtue of Compassion. It houses Castle Britannia, seat of the monarchy and the Great Council. The city is a major hub for commerce, with the largest bank in the realm. Britain sits at the heart of Britannia with roads connecting to all other major cities. The Britain sewers beneath the city are home to ratmen and criminals.

title: Moonglow - City of Mages
summary: Island mage city, Honesty, Lycaeum (Truth)
content: Moonglow is an island city dedicated to the study of magic and the pursuit of knowledge, representing the virtue of Honesty. It houses the Lycaeum, the house of the Principle of Truth and one of the great libraries of Britannia. The city is home to the Mage Guild and countless scholars. Moonglow's opposing dungeon is Deceit.

title: Trinsic - City of Honor
summary: Paladin city, Honor, heavily fortified
content: Trinsic is a heavily fortified city representing the virtue of Honor. Known as the city of paladins and honorable warriors, Trinsic maintains a strong military presence and is protected by thick walls. The city was attacked by Lord Blackthorn's forces during his rebellion. Trinsic's opposing dungeon is Shame.

title: Yew - Forest of Justice
summary: Druid city, Justice, Empath Abbey (Love), courts
content: Yew is a forest city representing the virtue of Justice. Home to druids, rangers, and the courts of law, Yew is where legal matters are resolved. The Empath Abbey, house of the Principle of Love, is located here. The city was attacked by Lord Blackthorn during his rebellion. Yew's opposing dungeon is Wrong.

title: Minoc - Mining Town
summary: Mining town, Sacrifice, artisans, mountains
content: Minoc is a mountain mining town representing the virtue of Sacrifice. Known for its artisans, craftsmen, and miners, Minoc supplies ore and ingots to blacksmiths across Britannia. The town sits in the mountains with rich mineral deposits nearby. Minoc's opposing dungeon is Covetous.

title: Jhelom - Warrior City
summary: Warrior city, Valor, volcanic islands, fighters
content: Jhelom is a city built on volcanic islands, representing the virtue of Valor. Known as the city of warriors and mercenaries, Jhelom celebrates strength, courage, and martial prowess. The Fighter's Guild maintains a strong presence here. Jhelom's opposing dungeon is Destard, the dragon lair.

title: Skara Brae - Island of Spirituality
summary: Spiritual island, Spirituality, meditation
content: Skara Brae is an island city representing the virtue of Spirituality, which combines all three Principles (Truth, Love, and Courage). The city is focused on meditation, inner peace, and spiritual enlightenment. Skara Brae has been destroyed and rebuilt multiple times throughout history. Its opposing dungeon is Hythloth.

title: Magincia - City of Humility
summary: Humility, destroyed by pride, rebuilt as New Magincia
content: Magincia was a once-proud city representing Humility. It was destroyed by daemons as punishment for the citizens' pride and arrogance, serving as a lesson about hubris. The city has been rebuilt as New Magincia, a humble farming community dedicated to the virtue it represents. The Shrine of Humility is located near the Stygian Abyss.

title: Vesper - Port and Mining Hub
summary: Port city, mining, trade, no virtue association
content: Vesper is a major port and mining city in northeastern Britannia. While not associated with a specific virtue, it serves as an important economic hub connecting sea trade routes. The city is known for smithing and mining operations, with access to rich mineral deposits in nearby mountains.

title: Destard - Dragon Lair
summary: Dragon dungeon, anti-Valor, ancient wyrms, Jhelom
content: Destard is a massive dungeon serving as the lair of dragons, representing the anti-virtue of Cowardice (opposite of Valor). Ancient wyrms, drakes, and lesser dragons nest here, guarding vast treasure hoards. It is one of the most dangerous dungeons in Britannia. Destard is associated with the city of Jhelom.

title: Deceit - Undead Dungeon
summary: Undead dungeon, anti-Honesty, liches, Moonglow
content: Deceit is a dungeon filled with undead creatures, representing the anti-virtue opposite of Honesty. Liches, bone knights, skeletons, and other undead inhabit its halls. The dungeon tests adventurers' truthfulness and integrity. Deceit is associated with the city of Moonglow.

title: Despise - Dungeon of Hatred
summary: Anti-Compassion, ettins, lizardmen, Britain
content: Despise represents the anti-virtue opposite of Compassion, filled with brutish creatures like ettins, lizardmen, and ogres. The dungeon embodies hatred and cruelty. It is associated with Britain, the city of Compassion.

title: Wrong - Dungeon of Injustice
summary: Anti-Justice, training dungeon, mongbats, Yew
content: Wrong represents the anti-virtue opposite of Justice. It contains relatively weaker creatures like mongbats and is often used as a training ground for new adventurers. Despite its name suggesting injustice, it serves an important purpose in preparing fighters. Associated with the city of Yew.

title: Covetous - Dungeon of Greed
summary: Anti-Sacrifice, greed, harpies, gazers, Minoc
content: Covetous represents the anti-virtue opposite of Sacrifice, embodying greed and avarice. The dungeon is filled with treasure-seeking creatures like harpies, gazers, and headless. Those who enter seeking only wealth learn the danger of greed. Associated with the mining town of Minoc.

title: Shame - Dungeon of Dishonor
summary: Anti-Honor, earth elementals, scorpions, Trinsic
content: Shame represents the anti-virtue opposite of Honor. The dungeon features earth elementals, scorpions, and other creatures dwelling in underground caverns. Adventurers must maintain their honor even when facing overwhelming odds. Associated with the honorable city of Trinsic.

title: Hythloth - Dungeon of Chaos
summary: Anti-Spirituality, eight levels, daemons, Skara Brae
content: Hythloth represents the anti-virtue opposite of Spirituality, embodying chaos and demonic corruption. The dungeon has eight levels (one for each virtue) and is filled with daemons, gargoyles, and other demonic creatures. It is one of the deepest and most dangerous dungeons. Associated with Skara Brae.

title: Gem of Immortality
summary: Mondain's gem, made him invincible, shattered by Stranger
content: The Gem of Immortality was a mystical artifact created by Mondain from his father's Sun Ruby. This gem made Mondain truly immortal and invincible. The only way to defeat him was for the Stranger to travel back in time using a time machine and destroy the Gem before its completion. When the incomplete Gem was shattered, it caused a catastrophic rupture in reality, sundering Sosaria into fragments and creating parallel timelines.

title: The Stranger (The Avatar)
summary: Hero summoned from another world, defeated evil, became Avatar
content: The Stranger is the legendary hero summoned from another world to save Sosaria and later Britannia. This hero defeated Mondain, Minax, and Exodus in succession. The Stranger later became known as the Avatar, embodying the Eight Virtues. The Avatar's adventures shaped the very destiny of Britannia and inspired countless others to follow the path of virtue.

title: Sosaria - The Ancient World
summary: Original world, four continents, shattered by Gem breaking
content: Sosaria was the ancient world consisting of four continents: the Lands of Lord British, the Lands of the Dark Unknown, the Lands of the Feudal Lords, and the Lands of Danger and Despair. When Mondain's Gem of Immortality was shattered, the catastrophic release of energy sundered Sosaria, splitting the continents apart. The Lands of Lord British became simply 'Sosaria' and later was renamed Britannia. The other continents were thought lost, though the Serpent Isle eventually reappeared.

title: The Three Principles
summary: Truth, Love, Courage - foundation of Eight Virtues
content: The Three Principles are the fundamental moral foundations from which the Eight Virtues are derived. They are: Truth, represented by the Lycaeum in Moonglow; Love, represented by the Empath Abbey in Yew; and Courage, represented by Serpent's Hold near Trinsic. These three Principles combine in various ways to form the Eight Virtues. Honesty comes from Truth alone, Compassion from Love alone, and Valor from Courage alone. Justice combines Truth and Love, Sacrifice combines Love and Courage, and Honor combines Truth and Courage. Spirituality requires all three Principles, while Humility serves as the foundation that makes all virtues possible.

title: Formation of Britannia
summary: Lord British's rise, Great Council, eight city unification
content: After the defeat of Exodus and the transformation from Sosaria, Lord British rose to power and united the eight city-states under a single banner. He established the Great Council to govern the realm and created the Eight Virtues system as the moral foundation of society. The realm was named Britannia in honor of Lord British. The eight cities - Britain, Moonglow, Trinsic, Yew, Minoc, Jhelom, Skara Brae, and Magincia - each represented one of the Eight Virtues. This unification brought peace and stability after the chaos of the Age of Mondain, Minax, and Exodus. The Great Council continues to rule in Lord British's absence, maintaining the laws and traditions he established.

title: The Age of Mondain
summary: King Wolfgang, Mondain's betrayal, reign of terror
content: The Age of Mondain was a dark period in Sosaria's history. King Wolfgang ruled over Sosaria and had two sons, Mondain and his brother. Mondain, consumed by ambition and a thirst for power, murdered his own father to obtain the Sun Ruby, a powerful artifact. He transformed this ruby into the Gem of Immortality, which made him invincible and immortal. With this power, Mondain enslaved all of Sosaria using dark magic, ruling with an iron fist. His reign of terror lasted until the Stranger was summoned from another world. The Stranger used a time machine to travel back before the Gem's completion, shattering it and destroying Mondain. This act caused the catastrophic sundering of Sosaria.

title: Transformation from Sosaria to Britannia
summary: Sundering after Gem shattering, continents split, Lands of Lord British becomes Britannia
content: The transformation from Sosaria to Britannia was caused by the catastrophic shattering of Mondain's Gem of Immortality. When the Stranger destroyed the incomplete Gem using a time machine, the release of magical energy was so immense that it sundered reality itself. The four continents of Sosaria - the Lands of Lord British, the Lands of the Dark Unknown, the Lands of the Feudal Lords, and the Lands of Danger and Despair - were split apart. The Lands of Lord British survived and became the primary realm, first called simply 'Sosaria' and later renamed 'Britannia' in honor of Lord British. The other continents were thought lost to time and space, though some fragments like the Serpent Isle eventually reappeared. This transformation marked the end of the Age of Mondain and the beginning of the Age of Britannia.

title: The Great Council
summary: Governing body, formed by Lord British, rules in his absence
content: The Great Council is the governing body of Britannia, established by Lord British to help rule the realm. The Council consists of representatives from the eight cities and various important figures. When Lord British mysteriously disappeared from the realm, the Great Council assumed full governance responsibilities, maintaining the laws, traditions, and the Eight Virtues system that Lord British had established. The Council meets at Castle Britannia in Britain, the capital city. They handle matters of state, justice, and maintain order throughout Britannia. The Council continues to rule in Lord British's absence, preserving his legacy and the moral foundation of the realm.

title: The Ancient Ones
summary: First society, defeated daemons, bound them to their will
content: The Ancient Ones were the first society to settle Sosaria, predating all recorded history. This mysterious race of beings possessed great power and wisdom. They faced a dire threat when daemons attempted to claim the world for themselves. Through their might and magic, the Ancient Ones defeated the daemons and bound them to their will. Some of the Ancient Ones ascended to higher states of being, transcending mortality, while others chose to become mortal and live among the peoples they protected. The legacy of the Ancient Ones shaped the very foundations of Sosaria, and their influence can still be felt in the deepest dungeons and most ancient ruins. Their victory over the daemons prevented the world from falling into darkness before recorded history even began.

title: The Codex of Ultimate Wisdom
summary: Sacred book, found in Stygian Abyss, enshrined on Isle of the Avatar
content: The Codex of Ultimate Wisdom is the most sacred book in all of Britannia, containing the fundamental truths of the Eight Virtues and the deepest questions of existence and morality. The Codex was discovered at the bottom of the Great Stygian Abyss, accessible only to those who had mastered all Eight Virtues. The Stranger, after completing the Quest of the Avatar, ventured into the Abyss, entered the Chamber of the Codex, and read its wisdom. After the Quest, the Great Council raised the Codex from the depths and enshrined it on the Isle of the Avatar. The geological instability caused by raising the Codex led to the formation of the Underworld. The Codex later became a point of conflict during the Gargoyle War, as both humans and Gargoyles claimed it. The Avatar ultimately sacrificed the Codex back into the Void to fulfill a prophecy and end the war, demonstrating that true wisdom comes from understanding, not possession.

title: The Quest of the Avatar
summary: Stranger's journey to master all Eight Virtues and become the Avatar
content: After the horrors of Mondain, Minax, and Exodus, Lord British realized that while people accepted the Virtues as a guide, they needed a living role model - someone who embodied the Virtues and could help others understand how to apply them in everyday life. He called forth the Quest of the Avatar, summoning the Stranger to become a spiritual leader by example. Throughout this great quest, the Stranger traveled to each of the eight cities, learned the meaning of each Virtue through actions and deeds, and visited the three Principles: Truth at the Lycaeum, Love at the Empath Abbey, and Courage at Serpent's Hold. The Stranger's actions determined how close they came to the ideal of virtue. Upon achieving enlightenment in every Virtue, the hero ventured into the Great Stygian Abyss to read the Codex of Ultimate Wisdom and became the Avatar - the embodiment of the Eight Virtues and a beacon of hope for all of Britannia.

title: The Shadowlords
summary: Three entities of Hatred, Cowardice, Falsehood, created from Gem shards
content: The Shadowlords were three malevolent entities representing the anti-virtues: Hatred (opposite of Compassion), Cowardice (opposite of Valor), and Falsehood (opposite of Honesty). They were created from shards of Mondain's Gem of Immortality that corrupted Captain Johne's three companions during a journey. These entities fed on negative emotions and could corrupt entire cities, possessing people and turning them evil. The Shadowlords threatened to corrupt all of Britannia, spreading despair and vice wherever they went. During Lord British's absence in the Underworld, the Shadowlords corrupted Lord Blackthorn, leading him to enforce a twisted version of the Virtues through draconian laws. The Avatar eventually destroyed the gems that housed the Shadowlords' essences, banishing them from Britannia. However, fragments of these gems may still exist, and even shards can spread corruption and despair. The Shadowlords' influence left lasting scars on the realm.

title: Honesty - The Virtue of Truth
summary: Truthfulness, Moonglow, Deceit dungeon
content: Honesty is the virtue derived from the Principle of Truth. It represents the pursuit of truthfulness with respect to oneself and others, requiring one to be honest in all dealings and to seek truth in all things. The city of Moonglow represents Honesty, housing the Lycaeum where the Principle of Truth is studied. Those who practice Honesty must be truthful in word and deed, avoiding deception and falsehood. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Deceit, which tests one's commitment to truth against the temptations of lies and deception.

title: Compassion - The Virtue of Love
summary: Empathy, Britain, Despise dungeon
content: Compassion is the virtue derived from the Principle of Love. It represents the quality of empathy, recognizing and sharing the feelings of others, and showing kindness and mercy. The city of Britain, capital of Britannia, represents Compassion. Those who practice Compassion must care for others, show mercy to enemies, and help those in need. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Despise, which embodies hatred and cruelty, testing one's capacity for compassion against the forces of malice.

title: Valor - The Virtue of Courage
summary: Courage, Jhelom, Destard dungeon
content: Valor is the virtue derived from the Principle of Courage. It represents the courage to uphold virtue even when facing threats, dangers, or overwhelming odds. The city of Jhelom, built on volcanic islands and known for its warriors, represents Valor. Those who practice Valor must face their fears, stand against evil, and protect the innocent regardless of personal risk. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Destard, the lair of dragons, which tests one's courage against the most fearsome creatures in Britannia.

title: Justice - The Virtue of Truth and Love
summary: Truth+Love, Yew, Wrong dungeon
content: Justice is the virtue that combines the Principles of Truth and Love. It represents the wisdom that perceives what is right and wrong, and the fairness to act accordingly. The city of Yew, home to druids, rangers, and the courts of law, represents Justice. Those who practice Justice must be fair in judgment, treat others equitably, and ensure that truth and love guide their decisions. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Wrong, which tests one's commitment to justice against the temptations of injustice and unfairness.

title: Sacrifice - The Virtue of Love and Courage
summary: Love+Courage, Minoc, Covetous dungeon
content: Sacrifice is the virtue that combines the Principles of Love and Courage. It represents placing the interests of others over one's own well-being, giving of oneself for the greater good. The mining town of Minoc, known for its artisans and craftsmen who labor for others, represents Sacrifice. Those who practice Sacrifice must be willing to give up personal gain, comfort, or safety for the benefit of others. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Covetous, which embodies greed and avarice, testing one's willingness to sacrifice against the temptations of selfishness.

title: Honor - The Virtue of Truth and Courage
summary: Truth+Courage, Trinsic, Shame dungeon
content: Honor is the virtue that combines the Principles of Truth and Courage. It represents the courage to stand for truth regardless of circumstances, maintaining one's integrity and principles even when it is difficult. The heavily fortified city of Trinsic, known as the city of paladins and honorable warriors, represents Honor. Those who practice Honor must keep their word, maintain their integrity, and stand by their principles even when facing dishonor or shame. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Shame, which tests one's commitment to honor against the forces of dishonor and disgrace.

title: Spirituality - The Virtue of All Three Principles
summary: All Three Principles, Skara Brae, Hythloth dungeon
content: Spirituality is the virtue that requires all three Principles: Truth, Love, and Courage. It represents concern for one's inner being and awareness of unity with all things, seeking enlightenment and inner peace. The island city of Skara Brae, focused on meditation and spiritual enlightenment, represents Spirituality. Those who practice Spirituality must balance truth, love, and courage in their pursuit of inner peace and understanding. The opposing anti-virtue dungeon is Hythloth, which embodies chaos and demonic corruption, testing one's spiritual balance against the forces of darkness.

title: Humility - The Foundation of All Virtues
summary: Foundation virtue, Magincia, Stygian Abyss
content: Humility is the foundation of all virtues, requiring none of the three Principles directly but serving as the base upon which all other virtues are built. It represents recognition of the worthiness of all beings and one's place among them, avoiding pride and arrogance. The city of Magincia represents Humility, having been destroyed by daemons as punishment for the citizens' pride and rebuilt as New Magincia, a humble farming community. Those who practice Humility must recognize their own limitations, avoid hubris, and see themselves as part of a greater whole. The Great Stygian Abyss, where the Codex of Ultimate Wisdom was found, is associated with Humility.

title: The Lycaeum - House of Truth
summary: House of Truth Principle, Moonglow, library
content: The Lycaeum is the house of the Principle of Truth, located in the city of Moonglow. It is one of the great libraries of Britannia, dedicated to the pursuit of knowledge and truth. Scholars and mages gather here to study ancient texts, magical theory, and the fundamental truths of existence. The Lycaeum represents the Principle of Truth, from which the virtue of Honesty is derived. It is a place of learning and enlightenment, where seekers of truth come to expand their understanding of the world and themselves.

title: Empath Abbey - House of Love
summary: House of Love Principle, Yew, abbey
content: Empath Abbey is the house of the Principle of Love, located in the forest city of Yew. It is a place of compassion, empathy, and healing, where the Principle of Love is studied and practiced. The abbey serves as a sanctuary for those in need and a center for understanding the bonds that connect all living things. Empath Abbey represents the Principle of Love, from which the virtues of Compassion, Justice, Sacrifice, and Spirituality are derived. It is a place of peace and understanding, where the power of love and empathy is cultivated.

title: Serpent's Hold - House of Courage
summary: House of Courage Principle, near Trinsic, fortress
content: Serpent's Hold is the house of the Principle of Courage, a castle fortress located near Trinsic. It serves as a military stronghold and training ground for warriors, where the Principle of Courage is studied and embodied. The fortress represents the Principle of Courage, from which the virtues of Valor, Sacrifice, Honor, and Spirituality are derived. Warriors and fighters come to Serpent's Hold to learn courage, face their fears, and prepare for the challenges that await them. It is a place where bravery is cultivated and the strength to stand against evil is forged.
